title: What To Do When Hooks Feel Weak
content: If your hooks feel weak, first use Outlier Finder to study what promises are winning in the niche, then use Ytuber Title And SEO Lab to tighten packaging, and finally use AI Studio to generate alternative opening angles or title concepts. Start with clarity and payoff, then add curiosity.

title: What To Optimize First When Growth Is Slow
content: When growth is slow, optimize in this order: topic selection, packaging, then publishing consistency. Outlier Finder helps you see whether your niche has active breakout formats, Ytuber shows whether your current packaging is under-optimized, and Content Planner helps you spot timing patterns only after the first two are addressed.

title: Best Workflow For Finding New Ideas In A Niche
tags: what should i do in this niche
content: For niche idea research, start in Outlier Finder with a broad query and minimal filters. Use Breakout Snapshot to identify the repeatable pattern, then switch to AI Research for interpretation, and finally move into Ytuber AI Studio if you want title ideas or scripts shaped for your own channel context.

title: Best Workflow For Transcript Research
content: Use Tools in Single mode when you want the cleanest transcript workflow. Load the URL, confirm transcript availability, choose the language if multiple tracks exist, and export the text before doing any downstream summarization or manual analysis. Batch and Playlist are better when you need the same export repeated across many public videos.

title: How Transcript Export Works
content: Transcript export uses the accessible subtitle tracks available for the video. If more than one language exists, choose the most useful track first. If a requested language is missing, switch to Any Available or a known subtitle code instead of assuming the video has no transcript at all.

title: How To Interpret Outlier Results
tags: what should i look at first
content: When interpreting Outlier Finder, read the strongest candidate titles first, then check the reason strings, then compare the Breakout Snapshot charts for age and duration clustering. If the same pattern appears across several high-scoring videos, it is more useful than any single top-ranked result.
